name: Victor Gaskin
abstract: Roderick Victor Gaskin (November 23, 1934 – July 14, 2012) was an American jazz bassist. Gaskin was born in The Bronx, New York and moved to Los Angeles in 1962.

concerts.label: John Mayall, Delbert & Glen, and Tranquility
Al Haig, Piano, and Jimmy Raney, Guitar

about_quotes.speaker: Wilson "Willie T" Turbinton
about_quotes.evidence: I got a chance at Cannonball Adderley and Victor Gaskin, Roy McCurtis, all guys who had serious experience... For them to embrace our music, it was just confirmation that we were going in a better direction.

about_quotes.speaker: Branford Marsalis
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer asks 'Victor Gaskin?' but the interviewee corrects him, stating 'Victor Bailey!' and proceeds to discuss his relationship with Victor Bailey, not Victor Gaskin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Branford Marsalis — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: I heard his band with Nat live at a festival in Cleveland in the late 60s, you know the band with Joe Zawinul and Victor Gaskin and Roy McCurdy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] What about people like Bob Cranshaw, Victor Gaskin?
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview
